_doc: The neutral-noun → per-platform-concrete map. The core (PROTOCOL.md, the roles, the checklists) names every act with a neutral noun; this one table resolves each to its platform tool. A reader who needs the concrete consults this; the prose stays neutral. Single home — never copy this mapping elsewhere. Adding a platform = add a column.

explore-a-codebase.opencode: task (read-only framing — no built-in Explore primitive; frame the instruction as 'find and read, do not write')
explore-a-codebase._doc: A read-only exploration sub-agent. On Claude Code the Explore subagent type is native (fast, read-only enforced at the harness level); on OpenCode there is no built-in Explore equivalent — a task with explicit read-only instructions is the closest analog (the model infers the constraint from the framing, not from the harness).

continue-a-sub-agent._doc: Resume an existing sub-agent. Optional — absent or null ⇒ fall back to spawn-a-sub-agent (no loss of capability, only the re-read token cost). Never use to continue a gate role (Reviewer): invariant 3 non-gate carve-out only.

the-enforcement-layer.opencode: tool.execute.before plugin (throw, deny) + chat.message plugin (push part, inject)

apply-config._doc: How setup APPLIES .ai-dev/config.json after writing it — re-assemble the role agents (and commands) so chosen models/agents take effect (docs/architecture.md `## Extension points`, the orchestrator's `## Setup` step 4). Per platform: run install-agents (+ install-commands when the command surface changed). OpenCode also has install-plugin — it GENERATES the deployed plugin (.opencode/plugins/ai-dev.mjs) from the source entry so the two cannot drift; re-run it when the entry changes (src/adapter/INSTALL.md, the OpenCode 'Enforce deny + inject' note). PATH is environment-dependent — in THIS repo the installers live under `src/adapter/`; in a downstream they live under the tooling submodule `.ai-dev/tooling/src/adapter/`. The commands below name the in-repo path; a downstream rewrites the one prefix, exactly like every wiring step in src/adapter/INSTALL.md.

class_support._doc: Which enforcement return classes each platform realises. A rule whose class is unsupported falls back to persona (recorded per-rule in deny-rules.json `fallback`). Claude realises all three (deny + ask via PreToolUse, inject via UserPromptSubmit). OpenCode realises deny (tool.execute.before, throw) and inject (chat.message — a per-turn part pushed before the LLM call, the analog of UserPromptSubmit); ask falls back to persona (no ask-return in the plugin hooks). Both OpenCode classes are live-verified on opencode 1.17.x: deny blocked a write into .ai-dev/tooling/, and inject fired on an unconfigured project — the no-config-run-setup nudge reached the model and the orchestrator offered setup.

models._doc: Per-platform model policy for the project config's role `model` field (.ai-dev/config.json). ZERO-CONFIG by default — works with no setup. `session` = the orchestrator's model. `auto` = a different model for independent blind spots IF the platform offers one out of the box, ELSE it falls back to `session` (never an error). Names are environment-dependent — that is why this lives in the adapter, not the neutral config. This block is also the home of the **list-available-models** contract point (docs/architecture.md `## Extension points`): per platform, HOW setup learns which models the environment offers — `discover` below.

claude.auto: the allow-listed model opposite the session's (opus<->sonnet) — always available, so cross-model review works zero-config
claude.discover: the known fixed pair above (`allow`) — no enumeration call; the model space on Claude IS this pair, so the allowlist is meaningful and gates a pin.

opencode.discover: run-a-shell-command `opencode models` and parse the `provider/model` ids it prints (one per line). If the command is absent or errors (older opencode, sandbox), fall back to a guided dialog where the Operator types/confirms the id — never invent one.
opencode._note: On OpenCode the ENVIRONMENT is the authority, not a static list: what `opencode models` reports (or the pin names) is what is usable, so `allow: []` is NOT a hard gate — it is an explicit 'no baked-in environment ids in the shared core' marker (env ids live only in a project's own .ai-dev/config.json). NO cross-model reviewer is realisable on OpenCode TODAY, even with a concrete pin: the `task` runtime parses a subagent's `model:` frontmatter but does NOT apply it at execution — a cluster of open upstream bugs (#21632 / #17870 / #18615; fix PR #14961 closed unmerged) with no fix through 1.17.7 (research: docs/decisions/opencode-task-capabilities.md Q1). So `auto`/`session`/absent AND a concrete pin all run on the one session model — no cross-model independence. The install-agents step therefore bakes NO `model:` line for any of them (silently swallowing the pin would otherwise claim independence it can't deliver); a recorded pin is kept in config so it auto-heals the day upstream fixes the cluster or the project moves to Claude — state to the Operator that it is inert today. Re-check at each release-audit (vendor-watch).
